task.title: Customize Published Documentation
task.shortdesc: Configure the appearance of your published Postman documentation by setting a custom logo, theme colors, and a custom domain.

prereq.p: Before you customize documentation, ensure that the documentation is already published. For instructions, see .
Custom domain configuration requires a Postman Basic plan or higher.

context.p: Published Postman documentation supports visual customization through the team settings in the Postman web dashboard. You can set a logo, choose a theme color, and configure a custom domain so the documentation appears under your own subdomain.

step.cmd: Sign in to your Postman account at postman.com and navigate to your team dashboard.
Select your team name in the upper-left corner and then select Team Settings.
Select Public Profile in the left navigation panel.
To add a logo, select Upload logo and upload a PNG or SVG image.

info.p: The recommended logo size is 400 x 400 pixels or larger. Postman displays the logo in the upper-left corner of the published documentation page.

step.cmd: To configure a custom domain, select Custom Domain in the left navigation panel.
In the Domain field, enter the subdomain you want to use for the documentation.
Follow the DNS configuration instructions that Postman displays.

info.p: You must add a CNAME record in your DNS provider that points your subdomain to the Postman documentation server. DNS changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate.

ol.li: Log in to your DNS provider's control panel.

Add a CNAME record with:
Host: docs (or your chosen subdomain prefix)
Value: The target provided by Postman, such as phs.getpostman.com

step.cmd: Return to Postman and select Verify Domain.
step.stepresult: Postman checks the DNS records. When verification succeeds, the custom domain becomes active and the documentation is accessible at your custom URL.

r.p: The published documentation displays your team logo and is accessible at the custom domain you configured. All existing documentation links automatically redirect to the new custom domain URL.
